(d) Counseling Services: Headed by a licensed counselor, the counseling program provides a supportive environment with a comprehensive and holistic approach to student well-being.

(e) Therapy Dog Program: The school therapy dog works with staff to provide comfort and support to students.
sections.simplePlain:
The school helps all students succeed and feel they belong:

(a) Cohorts and Advisories: Ninth graders are in groups of 12-14 with a teacher or staff member who meets with them every week. Older students have an advisor too. These groups meet weekly to talk as a community.

(b) Class Deans: Each grade has a dean who helps students with schoolwork and activities and is there when students need help.

(c) Academic Support: The Rand Learning Center helps students who need extra support with learning or organization. Older students also work as teaching assistants to help classmates.

(d) Counseling: A counselor is available to help with emotions and well-being. The counselor focuses on the whole student.

(e) Therapy Dog: The school has a therapy dog that students can spend time with for comfort and support.

sections.title: Community and Belonging Programming
sections.technical:
The school is committed to fostering a sense of Beloved Community, grounded in the vision of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., where the school is "based on justice, equal opportunity, and love for all fellow human beings, where all members are important and interconnected."

The school operates from a principle of Diversity as Excellence—research shows that diverse groups make better decisions, solve problems more effectively, and create more innovative solutions.

The goal of community programming is for all students to show up as authentic selves, express views openly with dignity, have their differences celebrated, and feel seen and valued.

(a) Affinity Spaces: Open to all students, affinity spaces focus on examining identities (race, gender, sexual orientation, nationality, religion, etc.), provide safe places to share experiences, build relationships, and develop positive sense of self.

(b) Gallard Fellowship: Since 1993, brings distinguished educators (typically from Morehouse College or Spelman College) to teach at Branson, instrumental in launching careers of young educators.

(c) Heroes & Holidays: Celebrates 100+ unique forms of ethnicity represented in the community. Students learn from others' cultural traditions and rituals.

(d) Recent Community Programming: "Beloved Community" event with Father Greg Boyle S.J., Black History Month assembly with Morehouse guests, Male Teen Conference, Recycled Orchestra of Cateura performance, Families of Color Social, Day of No Silence assembly, NorCal Innocence Project assembly, Lunar New Year celebration.
sections.standard:
The school is committed to fostering a sense of Beloved Community, grounded in values of justice, equal opportunity, and care for all community members, where all members are important and interconnected.

The school operates from a principle that varied perspectives strengthen the community—research shows that diverse groups make better decisions, solve problems more effectively, and create more innovative solutions.

The goal of community programming is for all students to show up as authentic selves, express views openly with dignity, have their differences celebrated, and feel seen and valued.

(a) Affinity Spaces: Open to all students, affinity spaces focus on examining personal identity and background (race, gender, personal identity and attraction, nationality, religion, etc.), provide supportive places to share experiences, build relationships, and develop positive sense of self.

(b) Distinguished Educators: Brings accomplished educators to teach at Branson, instrumental in launching careers of young teachers and providing role models.

(c) Cultural Celebrations: Celebrates the many cultural traditions represented in the community. Students learn from others' cultural traditions and rituals.

(d) Recent Community Programming: Community events focused on justice and belonging, Black History Month assembly, events supporting male students, performances highlighting marginalized voices, social events for families of color, student leadership assemblies, legal justice education, Lunar New Year celebration.
sections.simplePlain:
The school wants to create a "Beloved Community" where everyone feels they belong and matter. We believe that having people from different backgrounds makes our community stronger and helps everyone learn better.

We want all students to feel like they can be themselves, speak up, have their differences celebrated, and feel valued.

(a) Affinity Groups: Students can join groups based on their identity (race, gender, who they love, religion, etc.). These are safe places to talk about your experience, make friends, and feel good about who you are.

(b) Special Teachers: We bring accomplished teachers (often from historically Black colleges) to teach at Branson. They inspire students and show students what teachers of color look like.

(c) Cultural Celebrations: The school celebrates all the different cultures represented at Branson. Students learn about traditions and celebrations from many different backgrounds.

(d) Special Events: The school does events like celebrating Black History Month, gatherings for families of color, a day when LGBTQ+ students and allies speak out, events about justice, celebrations of different cultures, and more.

sections.title: Student Demographics Data Collection
sections.technical: The school shall collect and track data on student demographics including the following categories: race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status, geographic origin, financial aid status, prior school, primary language(s), religion, school type (public, private, international), and disability/accommodation status. Collection of demographic data shall be conducted in accordance with applicable privacy laws and with clear communication to families about the purpose and use of the data. Participation in demographic data collection shall be voluntary.
sections.standard: The school shall collect and track data on student demographics including the following categories: race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status, geographic origin, financial aid status, prior school, primary language(s), religion, school type (public, private, international), and accommodation needs. Collection of demographic data shall be conducted in accordance with applicable privacy laws and with clear communication to families about the purpose and use of the data. Participation in demographic data collection shall be voluntary.
sections.simplePlain: The school keeps track of information about students including their race/ethnicity, gender, whether they have financial aid, where they came from, what languages they speak, their religion, and if they have disabilities. The school collects this information to understand the school community and make sure all students are doing well. Families will know why the school is asking for this information, and providing it is optional.

sections.title: Admissions and Enrollment Equity
sections.technical: The school shall be committed to building a diverse student body through admissions and enrollment practices that do not discriminate on the basis of identity characteristics. Admissions decisions shall be holistic and shall not use demographic information as a barrier to admission. The school shall actively recruit students from underrepresented backgrounds and shall work to remove barriers to admission such as financial constraints. The school shall commit to making admission accessible through full need-based financial aid.
sections.standard: The school shall be committed to building a varied student body through admissions and enrollment practices that do not discriminate on the basis of identity characteristics. Admissions decisions shall be holistic and shall not use demographic information as a barrier to admission. The school shall actively recruit students from less-represented backgrounds and shall work to remove barriers to admission such as financial constraints. The school shall commit to making admission accessible through full need-based financial aid.
sections.simplePlain: The school wants to accept students from many different backgrounds. Admissions decisions won't be based on unfairly treating someone because of who they are. The school will actively recruit students from backgrounds that are less represented at Branson. The school will help remove barriers to admission like not being able to afford tuition by offering need-based financial aid.

sections.title: College Matriculation and Outcome Tracking
sections.technical: The school shall collect and analyze data on student college acceptances, enrollment, and outcomes, disaggregated by demographic categories including race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status (particularly tracking students receiving financial aid), and athletic recruitment status. Data shall be analyzed to assess whether college outcomes are equitable across demographic groups and to identify any disparities in acceptance to highly selective institutions or specific program areas. Such analysis shall inform efforts to ensure equitable college preparation and support.
sections.standard: The school shall collect and analyze data on student college acceptances, enrollment, and outcomes, disaggregated by demographic categories including race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status (particularly tracking students receiving financial aid), and athletic recruitment status. Data shall be analyzed to assess whether college outcomes are fair across demographic groups and to identify any disparities in acceptance to highly selective institutions or specific program areas. Such analysis shall inform efforts to ensure fair college preparation and support.
sections.simplePlain: The school keeps track of where students go to college, separated by race, gender, financial situation, and whether they're athletes. The school analyzes this to see if some groups of students are getting into better colleges than others. If there are unfair differences, the school works on ways to help all students prepare better for college.

sections.title: Alumni Tracking and Outcome Analysis
sections.technical: The school shall maintain demographic data on alumni cohorts tracked over time, including demographic categories of race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status, prior school, primary language(s), religion, school type, and disability status. Alumni data shall be analyzed to track college attendance patterns, college tier outcomes by demographic group, financial aid status, and long-term outcomes. Such analysis shall inform ongoing program evaluation and efforts to ensure equitable preparation and support throughout the student's academic journey at Branson.
sections.standard: The school shall maintain demographic data on alumni cohorts tracked over time, including demographic categories of race/ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic status, prior school, primary language(s), religion, school type, and accommodation needs. Alumni data shall be analyzed to track college attendance patterns, college tier outcomes by demographic group, financial aid status, and long-term outcomes. Such analysis shall inform ongoing program evaluation and efforts to ensure fair preparation and support throughout the student's academic journey at Branson.
sections.simplePlain: The school keeps track of alumni from past years, including their backgrounds and where they went to college. The school looks at this information to see if students from different backgrounds are having equal success in college. This helps the school see if it's helping all students equally or if it needs to do better for some groups.

sections.title: DEI Vendor Assessment Rubric
Food Purchasing and Dietary Equity
sections.technical:
The school's food service and catering vendors shall be selected and evaluated with attention to dietary equity. The school shall ensure that food offerings consider and accommodate: (a) cultural and taste preferences reflecting the diversity of the school community; (b) affordability for students receiving financial aid; (c) fair labor practices and organic sourcing where possible; (d) age-appropriate nutrition and healthy adolescent development; (e) gender-inclusive products and marketing; (f) sexual orientation and family structure diversity; (g) religious observances and dietary restrictions; (h) allergies and food sensitivities; (i) body size inclusivity and non-stigmatizing food culture; (10) language accessibility in menus and nutrition information; (11) sustainability and environmental responsibility; (12) nationality and international cuisine representation.

Food service providers shall be selected through a competitive process that includes DEI criteria, and the school shall regularly assess whether food offerings meet these equity standards.
sections.standard:
The school's food service and catering vendors shall be selected and evaluated with attention to dietary fairness. The school shall ensure that food offerings consider and accommodate: (a) cultural and taste preferences reflecting the varied backgrounds of the school community; (b) affordability for students receiving financial aid; (c) fair labor practices and responsible sourcing where possible; (d) age-appropriate nutrition and healthy adolescent development; (e) inclusive products and marketing; (f) family structure diversity; (g) religious observances and dietary restrictions; (h) allergies and food sensitivities; (i) body size inclusivity and non-stigmatizing food culture; (10) language accessibility in menus and nutrition information; (11) environmental responsibility; (12) international cuisine representation.

Food service providers shall be selected through a competitive process that includes fairness criteria, and the school shall regularly assess whether food offerings meet these standards.

sections.title: Vendor Equity Assessment Scoring Methodology
sections.technical:
Vendors shall be scored on each DEI/fairness criterion using a 4-point scale: (1) "Don't Know" - vendor has not addressed this criterion or the school lacks information; (2) "Early Awareness" - vendor has identified this criterion as important but is early in implementation; (3) "Developing Practice" - vendor demonstrates practices aligned with the criterion and gathers some data; (4) "Practice and Evaluation" - vendor demonstrates mature, well-documented practices with regular evaluation and continuous improvement.

Scores shall be weighted appropriately (with particular emphasis on living wages, healthcare access, and environmental responsibility) and a composite DEI score shall be calculated. This score shall be weighted as a meaningful component of vendor selection decisions, alongside cost, quality, and service reliability.
sections.standard:
Vendors shall be scored on each fairness criterion using a 4-point scale: (1) "Don't Know" - vendor has not addressed this criterion or the school lacks information; (2) "Early Awareness" - vendor has identified this criterion as important but is early in implementation; (3) "Developing Practice" - vendor demonstrates practices aligned with the criterion and gathers some data; (4) "Practice and Evaluation" - vendor demonstrates mature, well-documented practices with regular evaluation and continuous improvement.

Scores shall be weighted appropriately (with particular emphasis on fair wages, healthcare access, and environmental responsibility) and a composite fairness score shall be calculated. This score shall be weighted as a meaningful component of vendor selection decisions, alongside cost, quality, and service reliability.
sections.simplePlain:
Each vendor gets a score of 1-4 on each fairness criterion: 1 means we don't know or they haven't done anything yet; 2 means they've recognized it's important but are just starting; 3 means they have practices in place; 4 means they have strong practices and keep checking how they're doing.

The school puts extra weight on things like fair wages, healthcare, and environmental responsibility. All the scores get added together, and this fairness score helps decide which vendor to use, along with cost and quality.

sections.title: DEI Programming Budget
sections.technical:
The school shall allocate adequate and dedicated annual budget resources to support the DEI Program and related initiatives. Budget allocations shall support: (a) staffing of the Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion and supporting personnel; (b) professional development for all faculty and staff; (c) culturally responsive and inclusive curricular materials; (d) community-building and affinity group programs; (e) data collection, analysis, and assessment; (f) vendor selection and procurement for diversity, equity, and inclusion; (g) student support services including counseling, mentoring, and advocacy; and (h) regular external evaluation and consultation.

Budget allocation decisions shall be made in consultation with the Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion and shall be transparent to the community.
sections.standard:
The school shall allocate adequate and dedicated annual budget resources to support the Community Values and Fair Practices Program and related initiatives. Budget allocations shall support: (a) staffing of the Director of Community Values and supporting personnel; (b) professional development for all faculty and staff; (c) culturally responsive and welcoming curricular materials; (d) community-building and affinity group programs; (e) data collection, analysis, and assessment; (f) vendor selection and procurement standards; (g) student support services including counseling, mentoring, and advocacy; and (h) regular external evaluation and consultation.

Budget allocation decisions shall be made in consultation with the Director of Community Values and shall be transparent to the community.
sections.simplePlain:
The school will spend money each year to support this program for things like: paying the Director of Community Values and their team, training for teachers and staff, books and materials that show diverse perspectives, events that bring the community together, research about how we're doing, picking vendors fairly, helping students through counseling and mentoring, and getting outside help to evaluate how we're doing.

The Director of Community Values will help make decisions about the budget and will let the community know how the money is being spent.

sections.title: Staffing and Human Resources
sections.technical: The school shall maintain adequate staffing dedicated to diversity, equity, and inclusion work. Minimally, this shall include a full-time Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion reporting to the Head of School, with appropriate support staff. The school shall commit to hiring faculty and staff that reflect the diversity of the student body. The school shall conduct regular equity audits of hiring, retention, compensation, and advancement patterns to assess whether opportunities are equitable across demographic groups.
sections.standard: The school shall maintain adequate staffing dedicated to community values and fair practices work. Minimally, this shall include a full-time Director of Community Values reporting to the Head of School, with appropriate support staff. The school shall commit to hiring faculty and staff that reflect the varied backgrounds of the student body. The school shall conduct regular audits of hiring, retention, compensation, and advancement patterns to assess whether opportunities are fair across demographic groups.
sections.simplePlain: The school will keep a Director of Community Values and support staff to do this work. The school will actively try to hire teachers and staff from different backgrounds. The school will regularly check whether hiring, keeping employees, paying fairly, and promotions are fair for all groups of people.

sections.title: Budget Transparency and Dashboard
sections.technical: The school shall maintain a DEI budget dashboard that tracks allocation and spending across the program areas outlined in Section 11.01. The dashboard shall be reviewed quarterly by the Board standing committee and shall be shared with the school community on an annual basis. The dashboard shall include information on: (a) staffing levels and budget for diversity, equity, and inclusion personnel; (b) professional development spending and participation; (c) curriculum and materials budgets; (d) vendor DEI scoring and contract allocations; (e) student support services budget; and (f) comparative spending across school divisions to assess equitable allocation.
sections.standard: The school shall maintain a budget dashboard that tracks allocation and spending across the program areas outlined in Section 11.01. The dashboard shall be reviewed quarterly by the Board standing committee and shall be shared with the school community on an annual basis. The dashboard shall include information on: (a) staffing levels and budget for community values personnel; (b) professional development spending and participation; (c) curriculum and materials budgets; (d) vendor selection standards and contract allocations; (e) student support services budget; and (f) comparative spending across school divisions to assess fair allocation.
sections.simplePlain: The school will keep a budget dashboard that shows how much money is spent on this program. A committee will look at it every three months and the school will share it with families once a year. The dashboard will show things like how much is spent on the Director of Community Values, how much is spent on training teachers, how much is spent on books and materials, how much is spent helping students, and whether money is being spent fairly across different parts of the school.

sections.title: Annual Program Review
sections.technical: The Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion shall conduct a comprehensive annual review of the DEI Program and shall prepare a detailed report for the Board of Trustees and Head of School. The annual review shall include: (a) assessment of progress toward specific, measurable diversity and inclusion goals established by the school; (b) analysis of demographic data on student admissions, enrollment, and outcomes by demographic group; (c) analysis of faculty and staff hiring, retention, and advancement patterns; (d) feedback from students, families, and staff on sense of belonging and inclusion; (e) review of bullying and harassment reports and outcomes; (f) assessment of curriculum and classroom practices; (g) budget allocation and spending analysis; and (h) recommendations for program improvements.
sections.standard: The Director of Community Values shall conduct a comprehensive annual review of the Community Values and Fair Practices Program and shall prepare a detailed report for the Board of Trustees and Head of School. The annual review shall include: (a) assessment of progress toward specific, measurable goals for varied representation and belonging; (b) analysis of demographic data on student admissions, enrollment, and outcomes by demographic group; (c) analysis of faculty and staff hiring, retention, and advancement patterns; (d) feedback from students, families, and staff on sense of belonging; (e) review of bullying and harassment reports and outcomes; (f) assessment of curriculum and classroom practices; (g) budget allocation and spending analysis; and (h) recommendations for program improvements.
sections.simplePlain: Each year, the Director of Community Values will review the program and write a report for the Board and Head of School about: how we're doing on our goals, how many students from different backgrounds are being accepted and graduating, whether teachers and staff are from different backgrounds and getting promoted fairly, what students and families think about belonging at the school, how many bullying reports we got and what happened, how teachers are teaching, how money is being spent, and ideas for how to do better.

sections.title: Data Reporting and Transparency
sections.technical:
The school shall regularly share data on diversity and inclusion indicators with the school community. At minimum, the school shall publish an annual community report that includes: (a) current student demographic profile; (b) faculty and staff demographic profile; (c) admit rates and enrollment data disaggregated by demographic groups; (d) disaggregated academic and discipline data; (e) college matriculation data by demographic groups; (f) budget allocation to diversity, equity, and inclusion programs; and (g) progress on specific goals and next steps.

Data shall be presented in accessible formats and shall be discussed with transparency about both progress and areas needing improvement.
sections.standard:
The school shall regularly share data on community representation and belonging with the school community. At minimum, the school shall publish an annual community report that includes: (a) current student demographic profile; (b) faculty and staff demographic profile; (c) admit rates and enrollment data disaggregated by demographic groups; (d) disaggregated academic and discipline data; (e) college matriculation data by demographic groups; (f) budget allocation to community values programs; and (g) progress on specific goals and next steps.

Data shall be presented in accessible formats and shall be discussed with transparency about both progress and areas needing improvement.
sections.simplePlain: Every year, the school will share reports with families about things like: who attends the school and works at the school (broken down by background), how many students from different backgrounds got in and enrolled, academic and discipline data for different groups, where students went to college, how much money was spent, and what the school accomplished and what it needs to work on next.

sections.title: External Evaluation and Assessment
sections.technical: The school shall engage external evaluators on a periodic basis (at least every three years) to conduct independent assessment of the DEI Program. External evaluators shall review program implementation, data, practices, and outcomes and shall provide recommendations for improvement. Evaluations shall assess institutional commitment, the effectiveness of policies and practices, student and staff perceptions of belonging and inclusion, and progress toward program goals. Findings shall be shared with the Board of Trustees, Head of School, and school community.
sections.standard: The school shall engage external evaluators on a periodic basis (at least every three years) to conduct independent assessment of the Community Values and Fair Practices Program. External evaluators shall review program implementation, data, practices, and outcomes and shall provide recommendations for improvement. Evaluations shall assess institutional commitment, the effectiveness of policies and practices, student and staff perceptions of belonging, and progress toward program goals. Findings shall be shared with the Board of Trustees, Head of School, and school community.
sections.simplePlain: At least every three years, the school will hire outside evaluators to review the program and tell the school what it's doing well and what needs to improve. These evaluators will look at policies, data, what students and staff think, and whether we're meeting our goals. The report will be shared with the Board, Head of School, and families.

sections.title: Amendment and Continuous Improvement
sections.technical: These bylaws may be amended by the Board of Trustees upon recommendation from the Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion and the Board standing committee on community values. Proposed amendments shall be circulated to the school community for feedback prior to Board consideration. The school shall conduct regular policy reviews (at least annually) to assess whether policies remain current, reflect best practices, and effectively advance the school's commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. The school shall be responsive to feedback from students, families, and staff and shall use data and assessment findings to inform policy updates.
sections.standard: These bylaws may be amended by the Board of Trustees upon recommendation from the Director of Community Values and the Board standing committee on community values and fair practices. Proposed amendments shall be circulated to the school community for feedback prior to Board consideration. The school shall conduct regular policy reviews (at least annually) to assess whether policies remain current, reflect best practices, and effectively advance the school's commitment to community values and fair practices. The school shall be responsive to feedback from students, families, and staff and shall use data and assessment findings to inform policy updates.
sections.simplePlain: The Board can change these bylaws if the Director of Community Values and the Board committee recommends changes. Before the Board votes on changes, the school will ask students, families, and staff what they think. The school will review these rules at least once a year to make sure they still make sense and are actually helping the school live up to its promises. The school will listen to what students, families, and staff suggest and will use data to make improvements.
